findings.description: The core shape definition (rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite corner radii, used for containers/photos, modern/optimistic tone) has no corresponding rule at all. Only the email conversion-consistency sub-detail was extracted, leaving the foundational geometry/usage rule unclustered.

payload.description: Brand accent shape must be a rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite corner radii, other two corners keeping slight radius; used for callout text/photography containers.

findings.description: Approved print radius pairs (1.25"&0.0625", 0.5"&0.0625", 0.4375"&0.0625") are distinct governed values for the print channel and are entirely missing from the candidate rule set, which only covers email px conversions.

payload.description: Print radius pairs must be 1.25"&0.0625" (large), 0.5"&0.0625" (banners), 0.4375"&0.0625" (small callouts).

findings.description: The explicit exception for charts/tables (uniform 12px rounded corners on all four corners instead of the asymmetric accent shape) is unclustered. This is a distinct exception/override rule with its own governance tag [GENERAL: 12px] and should not be folded silently into the accent-shape rule.

payload.description: Charts and tables must use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (12px uniform radius, GENERAL), not the asymmetric accent shape.

findings.description: Rule lacks explicit governance facets: it omits the [GENERAL] channel tag, the exact px values per container size (120/6, 48/6, 42/6), and the default orientation clause (default: large radius top-left & bottom-right). As named it only captures the 'consistency' requirement, dropping the concrete conversion values that are the bulk of the source unit.

Source phrases the orientation as a 'default' (default: large radius top-left & bottom-right), implying it is a fallback rather than an absolute value, while the rule's overall hardness=hard_constraint/must does not distinguish between the strict consistency requirement (must be same orientation across sections) and the softer default starting orientation.

Missed relation: the chart/table exception (u_0004) directly overrides the base accent-shape geometry rule (u_0001); no relation is modeled between these since the base rule itself is missing from the candidate set.
